summary: All 18 business requirements are satisfied with code evidence, and the sole gap from the prior iteration (F1: the human gate-approval path did not push a live update) is now fixed. WHERE (R1) the detail page renders the summary in the header above the phase columns (app/views/pipelines/show.html.erb) and (R2) each list row renders a compact form that subscribes to the same stream (index.html.erb). WHAT IT SAYS: Pipelines::StatusSummary (app/lib/pipelines/status_summary.rb) is a pure, total derivation whose first-match ladder covers one active step naming phase/step/verb (R3), an iteration suffix only on the 2nd+ pass (R4, clause), two steps named (R5), three-or-more collapsed to a count (R6), awaiting-human with location (R7), completed (R8), failed with the phase/step where it stopped (R9), not-started (R10), canceled (R11), an unconditional non-blank catch-all so no state is ever blank or wrong (R12), and plain humanized language with no codes (R13). HOW IT STAYS CURRENT: Pipelines::BroadcastStatus is wired after-commit into StepRuns::Claim (start), Complete (finish/fail), RecordProgress (retry/progress), Phases::ManagerTick (gate starts waiting, escalation, convergence), and now Phases::Approve (gate stops waiting) — covering every event R14 enumerates; the broadcast reloads and re-derives so it is order-independent (R16); both controllers preload with_board so pages render true state on load without a socket (R15). LOOK/READS: status is carried by the sentence with the tone dot marked aria-hidden and only echoing a shared StatusHelper tone (R17), and a single shared partial/PORO drives both surfaces so full and compact forms can never disagree (R18). Integration, unit, and broadcast tests assert R1-R18 behavior.
